title: Cross-Fitted Beta-Calibrated Rank Ensemble
summary: The metric rewards ordering, while tree ensembles often emit distorted probabilities.
rationale: The metric rewards ordering, while tree ensembles often emit distorted probabilities. Calibration literature suggests beta calibration is a stronger binary post-hoc option than plain Platt scaling, and OOF rank blending is usually safer for AUC than naive probability averaging.
implementation_hint: Generate race-held-out OOF predictions from 2-4 diverse specialists such as the base classifier, LambdaRank model, and survival model. Beta-calibrate each on a nested calibration split, then choose blend weights on OOF AUC using weighted rank or logit-rank averaging; freeze that stack for test-time inference.
expected_effect: Likely a modest but robust gain, with less public-LB wobble than a single model or uncalibrated mean blend.
risk: Very easy to overfit blend weights or leak through calibrator fitting; nested OOF discipline is mandatory.
